perturbation.original_text: THIS AGREEMENT is made by and between , a corporation organized under the laws of , hereinafter called "Vendor", and CVS Pharmacy, In c., a Rhode Island corporation, and its subsidiaries and affiliates including Caremark Rx, L.L.C., hereinafter individually and collectively c alled the "CVS", effective as of the date set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: THIS AGREEMENT is made by and between , a corporation organized under the laws of , hereinafter called "Supplier", and CVS Pharmacy, In c., a Rhode Island corporation, and its subsidiaries and affiliates including Caremark Rx, L.L.C., hereinafter individually and collectively c alled the "CVS", effective as of the date set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Vendor" to "Supplier" in the introduction creates confusion because the rest of the document consistently refers to the first party as "Vendor." This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether "Supplier" and "Vendor" are intended to be the same entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party receiving any Information shall keep suc h Information confidential and shall not disclose such Information, in whole or in part, to any perso n other than its representatives who need to know such Information in connection with the receiving p arty's evaluation thereof and determination of business strategies, or other actions in connection with the Project (it being agreed and understood that such representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of the Information and shall be required by the receiving party to agree to treat the Information 2 - Revised May 7, 2010 confidentially), except with the prior written cons ent of the other party hereto or as otherwise permitted hereunder. Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Information on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by Vendor. Vendor will take all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals. Vendor further agrees to monitor networks, systems, and ph ysical plant for violations of this Agreement and to take the appropriate technical and procedural ac tions to ensure that any CVS Information is completely removed from any device, technologies, a nd/or locations in the event of any violation of security policy or procedure or any perceived infra ction thereof. Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Vendor" to "Supplier" in the introduction creates an in-text contradiction as defined by "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction". The contradicted text consistently refers to the first party as "Vendor". By changing the initial definition to "Supplier", the document introduces ambiguity as it is unclear whether "Supplier" and "Vendor" are intended to be the same entity, leading to potential confusion and disputes regarding obligations, especially in sections like the contradicted text, which outlines specific responsibilities for "Vendor".

perturbation.original_text: Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Information on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by Vendor.
perturbation.changed_text: The Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Data on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by the Vendor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data" in Section 4 introduces ambiguity. While 'Information' is defined in Section 2, 'Data' is not. It is unclear if CVS Data is the same as CVS Information, potentially limiting the scope of protection offered by the NDA. This creates an in-text contradiction within Section 4 itself, as it is now not fully clear what material the Vendor is prohibited from placing on unsecured devices.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party receiving any Information shall keep suc h Information confidential and shall not disclose such Information, in whole or in part, to any perso n other than its representatives who need to know such Information in connection with the receiving p arty's evaluation thereof and determination of business strategies, or other actions in connection with the Project (it being agreed and understood that such representatives shall be informed by the receiving party of the confidential nature of the Information and shall be required by the receiving party to agree to treat the Information 2 - Revised May 7, 2010 confidentially), except with the prior written cons ent of the other party hereto or as otherwise permitted hereunder. The Vendor is strictly prohibited from placing any CVS Data on portable computing/storage devices, which are not owned and secured by the Vendor. Vendor will take all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals. Vendor further agrees to monitor networks, systems, and ph ysical plant for violations of this Agreement and to take the appropriate technical and procedural ac tions to ensure that any CVS Information is completely removed from any device, technologies, a nd/or locations in the event of any violation of security policy or procedure or any perceived infra ction thereof. Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'CVS Information', which is a defined term. The changed text uses 'CVS Data', which is not defined. The contradicted text uses both 'CVS Data' and 'CVS Information', assuming they are the same. Because the Changed Text uses 'CVS Data', which is not a defined term, this creates ambiguity and thus a contradiction within the document according to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, specifically that the contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: Vendor will take all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor will take commercially reasonable measures, including encryption, to ensure that CVS Information stored on Vendor owned and secured devices cannot be accessed by una uthorized/inappropriate individuals.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures" with "commercially reasonable measures" lowers the standard of care. "Commercially reasonable" suggests a balance between cost and effectiveness, while the original wording suggests a more comprehensive obligation. This contradiction impacts the enforceability of the clause as it's unclear which standard applies, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purpose of complying with the obligations s et forth herein, the party receiving any Informatio n shall use efforts commensurate with those that such party employs for the protection of corresponding sensitive information of its own, and such receiving party shall not be liable for any inadvertent disclosure of Information provided that (i) it has used substantially the same degree of care to avoid disclosing such Information as it use s for its own information of like importance, and ( ii) upon discovery of any inadvertent disclosure it sha ll use reasonable efforts to prevent further disclosure of such Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text required 'all reasonable, necessary, and appropriate measures.' The changed text weakens this to 'commercially reasonable measures.' This introduces a contradiction with the 'contradicted text,' which requires effort 'commensurate with those that such party employs for the protection of corresponding sensitive information of its own' and 'substantially the same degree of care' as the party uses for its own information. 'Commercially reasonable' implies a cost-benefit analysis, potentially leading to a lower standard of care than the original text or the contradicted text, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor shall give CVS notice immediately if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor shall give CVS notice in a timely manner if CVS Information has been accessed by unauthorized individuals or if there is any evidence of any such infraction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "immediately" to "in a timely manner" introduces ambiguity. 'Immediately' is an exact term, and 'Timely manner' is subjective and undefined. This change contradicts the intention of prompt notification as initially stated in the contract, this creates uncertainty about what constitutes an acceptable delay.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damage s would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the other party s hall be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or forestall any such br each or threatened breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or i n equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'immediate' notice, while the changed text uses the subjective term 'in a timely manner.' The explanation states this introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about what constitutes an acceptable delay, contradicting the intention of prompt notification. While the contradicted text doesn't explicitly mention notification timing, it emphasizes the need for injunctive relief for any breach, implying urgency and immediate action, which is undermined by the vague 'timely manner.' Therefore, the changed text creates a contradiction because it weakens the urgency implied in the contradicted section by introducing a subjective timeframe where 'immediate' was previously required.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed a nd enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its choice of law provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed a nd enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its choice of law provisions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from New York to Delaware introduces a contradiction. Parties rely on specific state laws when entering agreements. This creates in-text contradiction in the contract as it changes legal frame and how will be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party understands and agrees that money damage s would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement and that the other party s hall be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy or forestall any such br each or threatened breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other rights and remedies available at law or i n equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from New York to Delaware. This creates a contradiction because the original agreement was designed and drafted assuming New York law would govern its interpretation and enforcement. Changing the governing law to Delaware could significantly alter how the 'injunctive or other equitable relief' mentioned in the contradicted text is applied and interpreted, potentially conflicting with the parties' initial expectations and understanding of their rights and remedies. Thus, the change creates an in-text contradiction in how this agreement will be enforced.
